creator: wforms@Safe-mail.net
text:
Something seems to be seriously messed up with the handling/detection
of optical disc drives in the 6.x series of FreeBSD (at least on intel
PII/PIII type hardware configurations).

I discovered this a few weeks ago when I tried to update a FreeBSD 5.4
system to 6.2R.

This is a VIA chipset Slot-1 PIII/800 system, with two 40 pin ide
connectors integrated on the motherboard. The system supports UATA133
mode with 80 wire cables. I have my 80 Gigabyte Samsung harddisk on
primary-master and there is no device installed for primary-slave (an
empty mobile rack is connected). Secondary-master is a Pioneer DVD-ROM
(read only) and secondary-slave is an older 52x speed LG CD-ROM (also
read only).

I had absolutely no problems under my old FreeBSD 5.4, but when I tried
to install 6.2R I had to realize that my DVD drive is not detected. I
asked help in comp.unix.bsd.freebsd.misc but no reply arrived for weeks,
so I am reporting this as a defect.

Please see my included dmesg outputs from various systems and see how
they detect the two optical drives!

All these dmesg outputs were generated on the same day and on the same
hardware using the default (GENERIC) minimal install, with the only
difference being the disk or partition (slice) where the actual test
system was installed and run from.
 
* FreeBSD 4.11R identifies both the DVD-ROM and the CD-ROM fine. I was
  able to do the installation from the DVD-ROM as acd0.
* FreeBSD 5.5R identifies both the DVD-ROM and the CD-ROM fine. I was
  able to do the installation from the DVD-ROM as acd0.
* FreeBSD 6.2R could not identify the DVD-ROM, but it identified the
  CD-ROM as acd0. The install CD did boot from the DVD drive and the
  first few steps of the installation seem to work, up until I select
  installation media as CD/DVD. After newfs running on the disk, reading
  selected packages from source fails. Interestingly, it also fails if I
  move the installation CD from the DVD drive (which was not detected by
  the system) to the CD-ROM drive (which is supposed to be acd0). The
  test system had to be installed via FTP.
* FreeBSD 6.1R and FreeBSD 6.0R both behave the same way as 6.2R. None of
  the 6.x series "see" the DVD-ROM drive, and they set the CD-ROM as acd0.
 
Another system, an IBM Netfinity 5000 server with dual Slot-1 PIII/500
also suffers from this defect. Hence the assumption that it may be
related to PII/PIII era systems.

The Netfinity 5000 has only one integrated 40 pin IDE connector with
an old-rubbish CD-ROM (read only), connected as master. The disks are
all SCSI disks using the integrated Adaptec adapter. When I boot from
the FreeBSD 6.2R install CD the CD-ROM drive does not show up in dmesg,
no acd0, therefore installation from CD is not possible. Again, this
also boots from the CD and gets to the main installation  menu, but
because no acd0 is detected installation from CD doesn't work. However,
boot CDs of Linux, NetBSD and OpenBSD (4.1) nicely detect the CD-ROM,
as well as FreeBSD 4.11R.
 
Since this optical drive issue is working fine with 4.11R and 5.5R but
not working with 6.0R, 6.1R and 6.2R, I assume that there is a little
screw-up in the 6.x series.

It would be nice to see this fixed in the upcoming 6.3R !
